references.title: Default code generation composition
references.why_read: Shows which model/resolver generators are included by default and where their config classes come from.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing generated artifact behavior or understanding why a script produces certain files.
references.what_you_learn: How the repository wires field resolver, directive, scalar, enum, object, union, and args generators together.

references.title: Core PSR4 code generation config
references.why_read: Shows supported template PHP versions, output namespace handling, and directory writability checks.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing output paths, namespaces, or PHP-version assumptions.
references.what_you_learn: The repository currently supports only the 7.4 template set through committed code despite wider PHP signals elsewhere.

references.title: Artifact generation and overwrite rules
references.why_read: Contains the main generation workflow, file writing behavior, and the logic that preserves edited object models when AutoGenerationInterface is removed.
references.when_relevant: Read before altering generation semantics, overwrite rules, or generated file saving behavior.
references.what_you_learn: How types, directives, scalars, and fields are generated and when php-parser is used to detect existing properties.

references.title: Full example regeneration script
references.why_read: This is the broadest committed example generation path and shows how federation v2.2 is combined with both code and TypeRegistry generation.
references.when_relevant: Read before regenerating example artifacts or tracing how SDL feeds the demo runtime.
references.what_you_learn: Which builders, schema utilities, namespace, and output directories are considered canonical for the example workspace.

references.title: Targeted TypeRegistry regeneration script
references.why_read: It is the narrowest path to regenerate only the example TypeRegistry and inspect generated resolver closures.
references.when_relevant: Read when debugging runtime wiring without changing all generated model artifacts.
references.what_you_learn: How the base TypeRegistry builder is invoked for the example schema and output namespace.

references.title: Example GraphQL SDL source of truth
references.why_read: All example generation and runtime behavior begins here.
references.when_relevant: Read first for feature changes expressed as schema fields, directives, unions, enums, scalars, or federation keys.
references.what_you_learn: Which types and directives exist, where federation @key is used, and which fields are expected to resolve at runtime.

references.title: Example HTTP GraphQL server bootstrap
references.why_read: Shows the full runtime path from generated TypeRegistry to StandardServer request handling.
references.when_relevant: Read when tracing runtime flow, container bindings, or manual HTTP reproduction behavior.
references.what_you_learn: Exactly which services are bound in the demo container, how Schema is instantiated, and how StandardServer is configured.

references.title: Example runtime walkthrough
references.why_read: Documents concrete HTTP requests and describes which resolvers and directives execute for them.
references.when_relevant: Read when reproducing runtime behavior or validating manual demo requests.
references.what_you_learn: How dynamicSum and _entities requests map to example resolvers, directives, and representation classes.

references.title: Canonical CI validation workflow
references.why_read: Defines what the repository actually validates in automation.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing Composer metadata, validation expectations, or cross-PHP compatibility behavior.
references.what_you_learn: PHP version matrix, Composer validation/install commands, php-parser v4 compatibility check, and the default PHPUnit command.

references.title: Root package manifest
references.why_read: It is the canonical source for dependency, package type, and autoloading information.
references.when_relevant: Read before making assumptions about supported runtimes, namespaces, or dependencies.
references.what_you_learn: The package is a Composer library, uses webonyx/graphql-php and nikic/php-parser, and autoloads src/, example/, and tests/.

references.title: PHPUnit suite configuration
references.why_read: Determines which tests run by default and which paths are covered in configured suites.
references.when_relevant: Read before choosing validation scope or adding new tests.
references.what_you_learn: Bootstrap path, coverage include path, and that configured suites cover builder, generator, and utils directories only.

references.title: Repository usage overview
references.why_read: Provides the highest-level explanation of purpose, example commands, and the AutoGenerationInterface customization rule.
references.when_relevant: Read first when you need broad context or user-facing usage expectations.
references.what_you_learn: Main features, demo commands, code generation commands, and how removing AutoGenerationInterface leads to field resolver generation.

references.title: SDL glob-to-Schema builder
references.why_read: This is the canonical schema loading entrypoint used by generation scripts.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing schema loading, split SDL behavior, or schema parse error handling.
references.what_you_learn: How glob() results are parsed and merged with BuildSchema and SchemaExtender, and how failures become SchemaParserException.

references.title: Federation v2.2 schema extender
references.why_read: Contains the actual federation aliasing and link-extension logic used by full example code generation.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing federation behavior or debugging _service/_entities and directive alias generation.
references.what_you_learn: How link, shareable, inaccessible, override, tag, and federation__* directives are added and when schema @link is injected automatically.

references.title: AMPHP TypeRegistry generation golden test
references.why_read: Shows exactly how async wrapping changes emitted TypeRegistry code.
references.when_relevant: Read before modifying AMPHP builder wrappers or generated async closures.
references.what_you_learn: Expected emitted code differences for the async builder path compared with the default path.

references.title: Execution-test schema and container setup
references.why_read: Mirrors the example runtime wiring without the HTTP server.
references.when_relevant: Read when keeping runtime tests aligned with example/start_graphql_server.php or when adding execution coverage.
references.what_you_learn: Which resolver, directive, scalar, representation, and union resolver services must be bound for the example schema to execute.

references.title: Example runtime execution coverage
references.why_read: This is the shortest executable proof of the demo runtime flow for dynamicSum and federation representations.
references.when_relevant: Read when tracing end-to-end runtime behavior or adding runtime-focused regression tests.
references.what_you_learn: Which queries are already asserted and how GraphQL::executeQuery is used directly against the example schema.

references.title: Federation v2.2 behavior assertions
references.why_read: Captures expected directive and _service/_entities behavior after schema extension.
references.when_relevant: Read before modifying federation helper logic or interpreting what extension behavior is intentional.
references.what_you_learn: Which directives and support types must exist after extension, including federation__ aliases.

references.title: SchemaBuilder merge behavior test
references.why_read: Provides the clearest proof that multiple SDL files are merged by glob iteration.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing SchemaBuilder or relying on split-schema behavior.
references.what_you_learn: What successful multi-file schema composition looks like in current tests.

references.title: TypeRegistry builder and resolver wrapper composition
references.why_read: This is the canonical place where resolver providers, directive wrapping, serializers, and default field resolvers are wired together.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing generated runtime closures, resolver lookup behavior, or directive wrapping.
references.what_you_learn: How the generated TypeRegistry runtime is composed and where federation/AMPHP variants hook in.

references.title: Generated example TypeRegistry output
references.why_read: It is the easiest place to inspect actual emitted runtime closures, directive wrapping order, and service lookups.
references.when_relevant: Read after understanding the builder when you need to see the concrete generated result.
references.what_you_learn: How fields, directives, union resolvers, scalar callbacks, _service, and _entities are encoded in generated PHP.

references.title: TypeRegistry class emitter
references.why_read: Defines the top-level generated class shape, getType cache behavior, and directive aggregation method.
references.when_relevant: Read before changing the outer TypeRegistry file structure or lazy caching behavior.
references.what_you_learn: How non-built-in types and directives become methods and what fallback Query/Mutation generation exists.
